ReadThisFirst: This file is the machine-readable step interface catalog, not a run-plan JSON file.
Use Steps as the only step contract list in this file; do not infer extra steps from prose or examples.
Use RecommendedExecutionOrder as the default ordering guide when assembling a new plan.
Use docs/cli/json-plan.md for the surrounding run-plan file shape and variable syntax.
Use examples/json-plans/*.json for complete plan examples.
AgentUsageRules: Select a step by exact Kind.
Supply every input with Required=true unless the plan intentionally obtains it from prior step outputs or defaults documented elsewhere.
Treat Preconditions as operational requirements that must be satisfied before the step runs.
Treat VerificationNotes as evidence requirements for judging success.
Do not use tsproj.merge-fragment or generic tsproj.upsert-* when a dedicated tsproj primitive exists.

If needed behavior is missing from Steps, report the missing human operation, XML before/after shape, target path, field meanings, verification method, and suggested API name instead of inventing a hidden step.
SyncPolicy: docs/reference/step-catalog.json and docs/reference/step-catalog.md are generated companions from the same catalog source.
Do not hand-edit either generated file.
Any change to step specs or generated reference wording must regenerate both files in the same change.
If the JSON and Markdown disagree, treat the generated pair as stale and rerun generate-docs; do not merge the disagreement manually.
For public step contract changes, update TwinCatStepCatalog.cs, implementation/CLI/tests as needed, then run generate-docs.

StepFieldMeanings.Kind: Stable public step identifier used in invoke-step and JSON plan steps.
StepFieldMeanings.MethodName: Service method that implements the step behavior.
StepFieldMeanings.Category: High-level step family such as engineering, tsproj, signing, or validation.
StepFieldMeanings.Preconditions: Required state or environment before running the step.
StepFieldMeanings.Inputs: Request fields accepted by the step payload or direct CLI options.
StepFieldMeanings.Inputs.Required: True means the caller must provide a value unless a wrapper supplies it explicitly.
StepFieldMeanings.Inputs.Example: Scalar example for that field; it is not a full step payload.
StepFieldMeanings.Outputs: Named values exposed by the step outcome and available to later JSON plan steps.
StepFieldMeanings.VerificationNotes: Evidence or readback expected after running the step.

PlanAuthoringRules: A run-plan file has variables/files/steps structure from docs/cli/json-plan.md; this catalog only describes valid step kinds and payload fields.
Each plan step should reference one catalog Kind and pass a payload matching Inputs.
Prefer small explicit steps over macro commands when durable evidence or precise repair is needed.
Keep deviations from RecommendedExecutionOrder intentional and explain them in plan comments or evidence.

Steps.Summary: Creates a C++/header/resource/None project item, optionally creating the physical file and registering it in .vcxproj and .filters.
Preconditions: The target C++ .vcxproj must already exist.
RelativePath must be inside the project directory.

Inputs.Name: RelativePath
Inputs.Description: Path relative to the C++ project directory.

Inputs.Name: ItemType
Inputs.Description: MSBuild item type, or Infer from extension.

Inputs.Name: AddToProject
Inputs.Description: Whether to register the item in .vcxproj.

Inputs.Name: CreatePhysicalFile
Inputs.Description: Whether to create an empty physical file.

Inputs.Name: AllowMsBuildFallback
Inputs.Description: Allow typed MSBuild XML update when DTE ProjectItems is not stable.

Outputs.Name: addedToProject
Outputs.Description: Whether .vcxproj registration exists after the step.

VerificationNotes: Verify the file exists, .vcxproj contains the requested Include, .filters contains filter mapping when requested, and reopen shows the item.

Steps.Summary: Removes a C++ project item registration and optional filter entry and physical file.
Preconditions: The target C++ .vcxproj must already exist.

Inputs.Name: RelativePath
Inputs.Description: Path relative to the C++ project directory.

Inputs.Name: ItemType
Inputs.Description: MSBuild item type, or Infer from extension.

Inputs.Name: DeletePhysicalFile
Inputs.Description: Whether to delete the physical file.

Inputs.Name: RemoveFilterEntry
Inputs.Description: Whether to remove the .vcxproj.filters item mapping.

Outputs.Name: deletedFile
Outputs.Description: Whether a physical file was deleted.

VerificationNotes: Verify .vcxproj/.filters no longer reference the item and the file is absent when DeletePhysicalFile=true.

Steps.Summary: Sets a C++ ItemDefinitionGroup tool property such as ClCompile include paths or Link dependencies.
Preconditions: The target C++ .vcxproj must already exist.

Inputs.Name: ToolName
Inputs.Description: Tool element name such as ClCompile, Link, ResourceCompile, or PostBuildEvent.

Inputs.Name: Value
Inputs.Description: Property value, including inherited macros when required.

VerificationNotes: Inspect ItemDefinitionGroup XML and confirm build logs use include paths, library paths, language standard, dependencies, or events.

Steps.Summary: Sets file-level MSBuild metadata for a C++ project item, such as PrecompiledHeader or ExcludedFromBuild.
Preconditions: The target item must already be registered in .vcxproj.

Inputs.Name: RelativePath
Inputs.Description: Path relative to the C++ project directory.

Inputs.Name: ItemType
Inputs.Description: MSBuild item type containing the item.

VerificationNotes: Inspect the item XML and confirm build output respects include/exclude or PCH metadata.

Steps.Summary: Sets a project-level or configuration-level MSBuild property in a C++ .vcxproj.
Preconditions: The target C++ .vcxproj must already exist.

Inputs.Name: PropertyGroupLabel
Inputs.Description: Optional PropertyGroup Label, such as Globals or Configuration.

VerificationNotes: Inspect .vcxproj and confirm the PropertyGroup contains the requested value; build log should reflect build-affecting properties.

Steps.Summary: Writes source/resource/text payload into an existing C++ project item file and returns its content hash.
Preconditions: The target C++ project must already exist.
ContentText and ContentFile are mutually exclusive.

Inputs.Name: RelativePath
Inputs.Description: Path relative to the C++ project directory.

Inputs.Name: ContentFile
Inputs.Description: Payload file path produced by the JSON plan files[] section or another explicit caller-owned payload.

Inputs.Name: RequireProjectRegistration
Inputs.Description: Fail if .vcxproj does not already register the item.

Outputs.Name: sha256
Outputs.Description: SHA256 hash of the written bytes.

VerificationNotes: Compare the output hash with the payload and reopen the item in VS.

Steps.Summary: Saves the current configuration archive when possible and then activates TwinCAT via ITcSysManager or DTE command fallback.
Preconditions: A solution must be loaded and its ITcSysManager must be available.

Inputs.Name: SaveConfigurationArchive
Inputs.Description: Whether to attempt SaveConfiguration before activate.

Inputs.Name: ConfigurationArchivePath
Inputs.Description: Optional override for the generated .tszip path.

Outputs.Name: activationCommand
Outputs.Description: The command or fallback path used for activation.

VerificationNotes: Confirm that either ITcSysManager.ActivateConfiguration or a DTE command succeeded.

Steps.Summary: Adds a TcCOM instance from a project TMC by resolving the module GUID and calling CreateChild on the project node.
Preconditions: The target C++ project and its .tmc file must exist.

Inputs.Name: ProjectTmcPath
Inputs.Description: Absolute path to the project TMC.

Inputs.Name: InstanceBaseName
Inputs.Description: Base name to use when creating the instance.

Inputs.Name: ModuleClassName
Inputs.Description: Optional module class name when the TMC contains multiple classes.

Inputs.Name: AllowOfflineFallback
Inputs.Description: Whether AddModuleInstance may inject a deterministic instance skeleton through .tsproj file mutation when COM CreateChild fails.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: TwinCAT tree path for the instance.

VerificationNotes: Export the instance XML and confirm that the expected CLSID and Name are present.

Steps.Summary: Applies a structured JSON module model to a TwinCAT C++ project .tmc without copying a whole known-good TMC file.
Preconditions: The target project .tmc must already exist.
GeneratedServicesHeaderPath should point at the project Services.h; GeneratedHeaderPaths can add companion headers such as Interfaces.h for custom interface GUIDs.

Inputs.Name: ProjectTmcPath
Inputs.Description: Absolute path to the project .tmc file.

Inputs.Name: ProjectName
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT C++ project / class factory name.

Inputs.Name: Modules
Inputs.Description: Structured module definitions containing GUIDs, interfaces, parameters, DataAreas, interface pointers, data pointers, and event classes.

Inputs.Name: GeneratedServicesHeaderPath
Inputs.Description: Optional generated Services.h path used to resolve custom type GUIDs and DataTypes.

Inputs.Name: GeneratedHeaderPaths
Inputs.Description: Optional additional generated headers used to resolve companion custom type or interface GUIDs.

Inputs.Name: LibraryName
Inputs.Description: Optional Library/Name override. Defaults to ProjectName.

Inputs.Name: RemoveUnexpectedModules
Inputs.Description: Whether module entries not present in Modules should be removed.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceDataTypesFromGeneratedHeader
Inputs.Description: Whether generated header DataTypes should replace the .tmc DataTypes section.

VerificationNotes: Run engineering.verify-tmc-data-areas afterwards and inspect the .tmc DataTypes/Modules sections; this step must not receive raw module XML fragments.

Steps.Summary: Runs SolutionBuild.Build and waits until the DTE build state reaches done.
Preconditions: A solution must be loaded in the DTE session.
VerificationNotes: Treat LastBuildInfo == 0 as the engineering success condition.

Steps.Summary: Closes the current Visual Studio DTE session, optionally issuing SaveAll first.
Preconditions: A live DTE session must exist.

Inputs.Name: SaveBeforeClose
Inputs.Description: Whether SaveAll should run before closing the session.

VerificationNotes: Confirm that the DTE process exits and the session object is no longer usable.

Steps.Summary: Creates a TwinCAT C++ project beneath TIXC using a specified Beckhoff wizard id.
Preconditions: A bound TwinCAT engineering session must exist.

Inputs.Name: WizardId
Inputs.Description: Wizard identifier for the project template.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: TwinCAT tree path for the C++ project.

VerificationNotes: Verify the TIXC child exists and the .vcxproj file lands on disk.

Steps.Summary: Creates a module class inside a TwinCAT C++ project via the module wizard id.
Preconditions: A TwinCAT C++ project must already exist.

Inputs.Name: AllowOfflineFallback
Inputs.Description: Whether CreateModule may patch module skeleton artifacts when module wizard automation fails.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: TwinCAT tree path for the created module node.

VerificationNotes: Confirm that the module files exist and the tree node is visible after save/refresh.

Steps.Summary: Creates a PLC project using the first compatible Beckhoff PLC template available on the machine.
Preconditions: A bound TwinCAT engineering session must exist.

Inputs.Name: AllowOfflineFallback
Inputs.Description: Whether CreatePlcProject may synthesize a minimal PLC project file mutation when COM/template creation fails.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: TwinCAT tree path for the PLC project.

VerificationNotes: Check that the PLC node exists and that the .plcproj file was created.

Steps.Summary: Creates a regular Visual Studio C++ project inside the current solution, such as an AdsClient console application.
Preconditions: A solution must already be loaded in the DTE session.
An installed Visual Studio C++ template or explicit AllowTemplateFallback=true is required.

Inputs.Name: ProjectDirectory
Inputs.Description: Optional project directory. Defaults to SolutionDirectory/ProjectName.

Inputs.Name: TemplateKind
Inputs.Description: Template semantic kind. P0 supports ConsoleApplication.

Inputs.Name: CandidateTemplatePaths
Inputs.Description: Optional explicit template paths for machine-specific VS installs.

Inputs.Name: PlatformToolset
Inputs.Description: Optional PlatformToolset value such as v143.

Inputs.Name: AllowTemplateFallback
Inputs.Description: Whether the service may synthesize a minimal MSBuild C++ project if no installed template is found.

VerificationNotes: Verify the .sln contains the project, .vcxproj exists, DTE can find it by name, and reopen keeps it visible.

Steps.Summary: Creates a fresh TwinCAT XAE solution and binds the automation session to its ITcSysManager root.
Preconditions: An active DTE session must exist.
At least one default TwinCAT XAE template path must exist on the machine.

Inputs.Name: SolutionDirectory
Inputs.Description: Directory where the solution should be created.

VerificationNotes: Check that the .sln and .tsproj files exist and that ITcSysManager can be retrieved.

Steps.Summary: Ensures a Visual Studio solution ProjectDependencies entry exists from one project to another.
Preconditions: Both projects must already exist in the saved .sln.

Inputs.Name: DependsOnProjectName
Inputs.Description: Project that must build before ProjectName.

VerificationNotes: Inspect .sln ProjectSection(ProjectDependencies) and reload the solution to confirm the dependency remains.

Steps.Summary: Creates or reuses a Task under TIRT and normalizes its timing, priority, and AMS port.
Preconditions: A bound TwinCAT engineering session must exist.

Inputs.Name: TaskSubtype
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT task subtype passed to CreateChild.

Outputs.Name: treeItemPath
Outputs.Description: TwinCAT tree path for the task.

VerificationNotes: Read back ProduceXml(true) and confirm TaskDef/Context values were updated.

Steps.Summary: Exports ProduceXml output for any TwinCAT tree node as durable evidence.
Preconditions: A bound TwinCAT engineering session must exist.
The target tree path must be valid.

Inputs.Name: TreeItemPath
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT tree path such as TIXC^MyProject.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the XML file and verify it contains the expected node metadata.

Steps.Summary: Starts a new DTE session that later steps can use for XAE creation, build, and activation.
Preconditions: Visual Studio with TwinCAT XAE must be installed on the machine.

Inputs.Name: StartupDelayMs
Inputs.Description: Warm-up delay before the DTE session is used.

Inputs.Name: Visible
Inputs.Description: Whether the launched Visual Studio window should be visible.

Outputs.Name: session
Outputs.Description: Live DTE session stored in pipeline state.

VerificationNotes: Read back DTE version or solution state to confirm the COM server is reachable.

Steps.Summary: Re-opens an existing TwinCAT solution and re-attaches COM references after .tsproj file mutations.
Preconditions: An active DTE session must exist.
The target .sln and .tsproj must already exist.
VerificationNotes: Confirm that the project can be found again in the DTE solution model.

Steps.Summary: Invokes the TwinCAT C++ project PublishModules method so updated module source regenerates TMC metadata.
Preconditions: The target TwinCAT C++ project must exist and expose PublishModules in its tree XML.

Inputs.Name: WaitForUpdatedTmcTimeoutMs
Inputs.Description: Maximum wait for the project .tmc timestamp to update.

Inputs.Name: RunTmcCodeGeneratorFirst
Inputs.Description: Whether to invoke StartTmcCodeGenerator before PublishModules.

Outputs.Name: succeeded
Outputs.Description: Whether publish left a readable project .tmc.

Outputs.Name: updated
Outputs.Description: Whether the .tmc timestamp or content changed during this publish call.

VerificationNotes: Check that the .tmc is readable and contains the expected module classes; updated=true means the timestamp or content changed during this publish call.

Steps.Summary: Flushes pending Visual Studio document and solution changes before build, reopen, or .tsproj file mutation steps.
Preconditions: A live DTE session must exist.
VerificationNotes: Re-check file timestamps or solution dirtiness after the command.

Steps.Summary: Invokes the TwinCAT C++ project StartTmcCodeGenerator method so source annotations regenerate TMC metadata.
Preconditions: The target TwinCAT C++ project must exist and expose StartTmcCodeGenerator in its tree XML.

Inputs.Name: WaitForUpdatedTmcTimeoutMs
Inputs.Description: Maximum wait for the project .tmc timestamp to update.

Outputs.Name: updatedTmcPath
Outputs.Description: Project .tmc path observed after code generation.

Outputs.Name: succeeded
Outputs.Description: Whether code generation left a readable project .tmc.

Outputs.Name: updated
Outputs.Description: Whether the .tmc timestamp or content changed during this code generation call.

VerificationNotes: Check that the .tmc is readable and contains source-derived DataAreas, Parameters, Interfaces, and module classes; updated=false can still be acceptable if content was already current.

Steps.Summary: Reads a TwinCAT C++ .tmc file and verifies expected module DataAreas and symbols before instances are created.
Preconditions: Run engineering.start-tmc-code-generator and engineering.publish-modules first when source annotations were just written.

Inputs.Name: ProjectTmcPath
Inputs.Description: Absolute path to the project .tmc file.

Inputs.Name: Modules
Inputs.Description: Expected module names, DataAreas, AreaTypes, and symbols.

Inputs.Name: FailOnUnexpectedModule
Inputs.Description: Whether modules not listed in Modules should fail verification.

Outputs.Name: expectedModuleCount
Outputs.Description: Expected module count from the request.

Outputs.Name: errorsJson
Outputs.Description: Detailed mismatch list when verification fails.

VerificationNotes: Use this after C++ code generation and before add-module-instance; a fallback skeleton TMC with Input/DataIn and Output/DataOut should fail this step.

Steps.Summary: Grants or removes local TcSignTool authorization for a TwinCAT signing certificate.
Preconditions: TwinCAT SDK TcSignTool.exe must be installed.
The certificate file must exist on this machine.

Inputs.Name: Password
Inputs.Description: Certificate password. Prefer CLI password-file or password-env-var input; it is never emitted in step outputs.

Inputs.Name: RemoveGrant
Inputs.Description: Remove the local grant instead of adding it.

Outputs.Name: commandLine
Outputs.Description: Redacted command line used for evidence.

VerificationNotes: Run signing.sign-twincat-binary and signing.verify-twincat-binary after grant to close the loop.

Steps.Summary: Writes TwinCAT C++ project signing license settings used by MSBuild/TcSignTool.
Preconditions: The C++ .vcxproj file must exist.
Run this before engineering.build-solution so the generated .tmx is signed during build.

Inputs.Name: ProjectFilePath
Inputs.Description: Path to the C++ .vcxproj file, or resolve it from ProjectPath and CppProjectName.

Inputs.Name: LicenseName
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT signing certificate/license name written to TcSignTwinCatCertName.

Inputs.Name: Password
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT signing certificate/license password written to TcSignTwinCatCertPW. Prefer password-file or password-env-var outside demo plans.

Inputs.Name: EnableSigning
Inputs.Description: Write TcSignTwinCat=true so TwinCAT MSBuild signs the output.

Outputs.Name: passwordWritten
Outputs.Description: Whether a password value was written; the password itself is never emitted.

VerificationNotes: Inspect the C++ .vcxproj or generated TcSignTwinCatCmd.txt after build; do not expect ADS/runtime state to change until the project is rebuilt and activated.

Steps.Summary: Signs one or more built TwinCAT C++ binaries with Beckhoff TcSignTool.
Preconditions: engineering.build-solution must have produced the target .tmx/.sys binary.
The certificate file must exist on this machine.

Inputs.Name: TargetPaths
Inputs.Description: One or more TwinCAT binary paths to sign.

Inputs.Name: Password
Inputs.Description: Certificate password. Prefer CLI password-file or password-env-var input; it is piped to TcSignTool instead of placed on the process command line.

Outputs.Name: commandLine
Outputs.Description: Redacted command line used for evidence.

VerificationNotes: Run signing.verify-twincat-binary and then activate the configuration with the signed binary.

Steps.Summary: Verifies that one or more TwinCAT binaries carry a valid TcSignTool signature.
Preconditions: The target TwinCAT binary must already exist.

Inputs.Name: TargetPaths
Inputs.Description: One or more TwinCAT binary paths to verify.

Inputs.Name: AllowTestModeWarning
Inputs.Description: Treat TcSignTool exit code 2 as success when the output says the file has a signature but the OEM certificate is not signed by Beckhoff.

Outputs.Name: acceptedTestModeWarning
Outputs.Description: Whether a TcSignTool test-mode certificate warning was accepted.

Outputs.Name: commandLine
Outputs.Description: Redacted command line used for evidence.

VerificationNotes: Treat exit code 0 as signed/verified; for local test certificates, set AllowTestModeWarning only when test-mode activation is acceptable.
Keep the signed binary path as activation evidence.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch of instance data pointer writes in one deterministic .tsproj mutation pass.
Preconditions: Each referenced instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: Items
Inputs.Description: Batch entries containing InstanceName, PointerName, ObjectId, AreaNo, ByteOffset, ByteSize, and optional ArrayIndex for array DataPointerValues.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify DataPointerValues entries for each requested instance/pointer pair.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch of instance interface pointer writes in one deterministic .tsproj mutation pass.
Preconditions: Each referenced instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: Items
Inputs.Description: Batch entries containing InstanceName, PointerName, and ObjectId.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify InterfacePointerValues entries for each requested instance/pointer pair.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch of instance parameter writes in one deterministic .tsproj mutation pass.
Preconditions: Each referenced instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: Items
Inputs.Description: Batch entries containing InstanceName, ParameterName, and optional Value/Enum/String payloads.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm each target instance received the expected ParameterValues updates.

Steps.Summary: Applies a batch IO topology payload by orchestrating dedicated IO Device, Box, PDO, MappingInfo, and Link primitives.
Preconditions: The payload must use structured dedicated fields and may only use documented raw fragments for known-good XML gaps.
Device entries must precede Boxes logically; the service applies Devices, Boxes, BoxImages, Pdos, MappingInfos, then Links.

Inputs.Name: EnsureIoSection
Inputs.Description: Whether Project/Io should be created before applying the plan.

Outputs.Name: deviceCount
Outputs.Description: Number of Device entries applied from the payload.

Outputs.Name: boxCount
Outputs.Description: Number of Box entries applied from the payload.

Outputs.Name: pdoCount
Outputs.Description: Number of PDO entries applied from the payload.

Outputs.Name: boxImageCount
Outputs.Description: Number of Box image updates applied from the payload.

Outputs.Name: mappingInfoCount
Outputs.Description: Number of MappingInfo entries applied from the payload.

Outputs.Name: linkCount
Outputs.Description: Number of Link entries applied from the payload.

VerificationNotes: Re-open XAE and compare normalized Project/Io and root Mappings snapshots; for disabled hardware topologies, build plus normalized XML is the first acceptance gate.

Steps.Summary: Applies generic element and fragment upserts in one deterministic .tsproj mutation pass.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
Use this for JSON-owned low-level mutations that do not yet have a dedicated primitive.

Inputs.Name: ElementUpserts
Inputs.Description: Element upserts to apply in order.

Inputs.Name: FragmentUpserts
Inputs.Description: Fragment upserts to apply in order.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify all requested low-level mutations landed; FailOnConflict aborts the plan.

Steps.Summary: Writes Instance TmcDesc Context/ManualConfig binding with configurable context id/name and CyclicCaller behavior.
Preconditions: The target instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: ContextId
Inputs.Description: Context Id value used for lookup/create.

Inputs.Name: ContextName
Inputs.Description: Optional Context Name value to enforce.

Inputs.Name: RemoveCyclicCallerWhenExcluded
Inputs.Description: Whether to remove CyclicCaller when IncludeCyclicCaller is false.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm Context Id/ManualConfig/OTCID plus optional CyclicCaller state.

Steps.Summary: Writes ManualConfig/OTCID and context timing values back into an instance TmcDesc, optionally also updating CyclicCaller.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
The task ObjectId must already be known.
VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj or produce instance XML again and confirm OTCID/CycleTime/Priority are present.

Steps.Summary: Writes PLC instance Context/ManualConfig binding so PLC execution context tracks an explicit task object id.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project and PLC Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

Inputs.Name: PlcTaskName
Inputs.Description: PLC task name written into Context/Name.

Inputs.Name: CycleTimeNs
Inputs.Description: PLC context cycle time in nanoseconds.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the PLC instance Context/ManualConfig contains OTCID/Priority/CycleTime.

Steps.Summary: Clears DataPointerValues under a C++ instance TmcDesc before activation or before applying a known-good data pointer plan.
Preconditions: The referenced instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: RemoveContainerWhenEmpty
Inputs.Description: Remove the DataPointerValues container instead of leaving it empty.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm stale DataPointerValues entries are gone before activation.

Steps.Summary: Clears ParameterValues under a C++ instance TmcDesc before applying a deterministic parameter plan.
Preconditions: The referenced instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: RemoveContainerWhenEmpty
Inputs.Description: Remove the ParameterValues container instead of leaving it empty.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm stale ParameterValues entries are gone before new values are applied.

Steps.Summary: Removes all root-level Mappings sections so mapping links can be rebuilt from a known empty state.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify root-level Mappings nodes are absent.

Steps.Summary: Clears all InitSymbol entries under a PLC Instance InitSymbols section.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project and PLC Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

Inputs.Name: RemoveContainerWhenEmpty
Inputs.Description: Whether empty InitSymbols container should be removed.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the PLC instance has no InitSymbol entries.

Steps.Summary: Removes all Vars groups under a PLC Instance node so PLC variable layout can be rebuilt deterministically.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project and PLC Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the PLC instance contains no Vars child elements.

Steps.Summary: Clears all TaskPouOid entries under a PLC Instance TaskPouOids section.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project and PLC Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

Inputs.Name: RemoveContainerWhenEmpty
Inputs.Description: Whether empty TaskPouOids container should be removed.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the PLC instance has no TaskPouOid entries.

Steps.Summary: Removes Vars and/or Image child nodes from a Task so task layout can be rebuilt deterministically.
Preconditions: The target Task node must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: RemoveVars
Inputs.Description: Whether to remove all Vars groups under the task.

Inputs.Name: RemoveImage
Inputs.Description: Whether to remove all Image entries under the task.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the selected child node categories were removed from the target Task.

Steps.Summary: Removes stale UnrestoredVarLinks blocks so unresolved TwinCAT links do not survive into activation.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify no UnrestoredVarLinks nodes remain before rebuilding mappings.

Steps.Summary: Ensures a named C++ Instance node exists under Cpp/Project and carries a minimal TmcDesc skeleton.
Preconditions: The target C++ Project must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: CppProjectName
Inputs.Description: C++ project node name under Cpp.

Inputs.Name: ContextId
Inputs.Description: Context Id value used for lookup/create.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify Cpp/Project/Instance plus TmcDesc/Contexts/ParameterValues/InterfacePointerValues/DataPointerValues exist.

Steps.Summary: Writes or updates a DataPointerValues entry beneath an instance TmcDesc.
Preconditions: The target instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: ByteSize
Inputs.Description: Byte width of the pointed segment.

Inputs.Name: ArrayIndex
Inputs.Description: Optional array index. When set, writes a Data child with ArrayIndex under the named DataPointerValues entry.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the named DataPointerValues/Value entry matches the requested offsets.

Steps.Summary: Creates or updates an EtherCAT Box under a Device or parent Box, preserving nested topology.
Preconditions: The target Device must already exist.
ParentBoxId, when set, must identify exactly one existing Box under that Device.

Inputs.Name: ParentBoxId
Inputs.Description: Optional parent Box Id for nested terminals or safety modules.

Inputs.Name: Disabled
Inputs.Description: Optional Disabled attribute. false removes the attribute.

Inputs.Name: EtherCatAttributes
Inputs.Description: Structured attributes for the Box/EtherCAT child.

Inputs.Name: EtherCatChildValues
Inputs.Description: Simple child values for Box/EtherCAT, such as SyncMan or Fmmu only when their meaning is known.

Inputs.Name: ExtraFragments
Inputs.Description: Known-good extra Box child fragments with required source/meaning/evidence metadata.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the Box appears under the expected Device/parent Box with correct Id, Name, ImageId, and EtherCAT metadata.

Steps.Summary: Ensures a PLC InitSymbol exists and writes Data from the provided ObjectId using TwinCAT little-endian encoding.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project and PLC Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

Inputs.Name: ObjectId
Inputs.Description: ObjectId used to produce InitSymbol Data, for example #x02010010.

Inputs.Name: TypeGuid
Inputs.Description: GUID attribute written on InitSymbol Type.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm InitSymbols contains the requested symbol name/type/area/data.

Steps.Summary: Writes or updates an InterfacePointerValues entry beneath an instance TmcDesc.
Preconditions: The target instance must already exist in the .tsproj.
VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the named InterfacePointerValues/Value entry contains the OTCID.

Steps.Summary: Creates or updates a Box ImageId and optional image metadata without replacing the Box.
Preconditions: The target Device and Box must already exist.

Inputs.Name: MetadataValues
Inputs.Description: Optional simple metadata child values written under the Box.

Inputs.Name: MetadataFragments
Inputs.Description: Known-good image metadata fragments with required source/meaning/evidence metadata.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the target Box keeps its topology while ImageId and requested image metadata are present.

Steps.Summary: Creates or updates a Project/Io Device with structured TwinCAT IO identity fields.
Preconditions: Project/Io will be created if missing.
Raw AddressInfo or extra fragments require source, parent path, field meaning, and verification evidence.

Inputs.Name: DevType
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT Device DevType, for example 109 for RT-Ethernet Adapter or 111 for EtherCAT.

Inputs.Name: Disabled
Inputs.Description: Optional Disabled attribute. false removes the attribute.

Inputs.Name: AddressInfo
Inputs.Description: Structured TcCom/Pnp AddressInfo or documented raw AddressInfo XML.

Inputs.Name: Images
Inputs.Description: Optional direct Image children for process images.

Inputs.Name: ExtraFragments
Inputs.Description: Known-good extra Device child fragments with required source/meaning/evidence metadata.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the Device Id, Name, DevType, AMS fields, AddressInfo, and direct Image children match the requested topology.

Steps.Summary: Creates or updates a mapping OwnerA/OwnerB/Link with optional IO/TcCOM mapping attributes.
Preconditions: Owner names and Var paths must match the IO/PDO or TcCOM paths visible to XAE.
Use structured LinkAttributes for Size, RestoreInfo, GrpA, TypeA, InOutA, GuidA, and similar known mapping metadata.

Inputs.Name: OwnerAName
Inputs.Description: OwnerA Name attribute, for example TIID^Device 3 (EtherCAT).

Inputs.Name: OwnerBName
Inputs.Description: OwnerB Name attribute, for example TIXC^MotionControl^BeckhoffDriver1.

Inputs.Name: LinkAttributes
Inputs.Description: Optional additional Link attributes such as Size, RestoreInfo, GrpA, TypeA, InOutA, or GuidA.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceExistingAttributes
Inputs.Description: Whether LinkAttributes overwrite existing attribute values.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify Mappings contains the exact OwnerA/OwnerB/Link path and any requested Size/RestoreInfo/GuidA metadata.

Steps.Summary: Creates or updates a Box/EtherCAT Pdo and its Entry children using structured PDO fields.
Preconditions: The target Device and Box must already exist.
Use ExtraFragments only for known-good PDO child XML with documented field meanings and evidence.

Inputs.Name: BoxId
Inputs.Description: Owning Box Id under the Device.

Inputs.Name: Index
Inputs.Description: Pdo Index attribute, for example #x1a00 or #x1600.

Inputs.Name: Entries
Inputs.Description: PDO Entry definitions containing name/index/sub/type and optional attributes/child values.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceExistingEntries
Inputs.Description: Whether existing Entry children are replaced before Entries are applied.

Inputs.Name: ExtraFragments
Inputs.Description: Known-good extra Pdo child fragments with required source/meaning/evidence metadata.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the target Box/EtherCAT/Pdo entries have the expected Index/Sub/Type/BitLen fields and mapping-visible names.

Steps.Summary: Ensures the root Project/Io section exists without replacing other Project children.
Preconditions: A root-level Project node must exist in the .tsproj.

Outputs.Name: created
Outputs.Description: Whether Project/Io was created by this call.

Outputs.Name: deviceCount
Outputs.Description: Number of direct Device children currently under Project/Io.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify Project/Io exists and existing Project/System/Plc/Cpp children remain intact.

Steps.Summary: Ensures IO Task Image structure on a task and binds the instance IoTaskImage pointer to the derived or specified Image ObjectId.
Preconditions: The target Task and instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: ImageId
Inputs.Description: Task Image Id used to derive the default ObjectId.

Inputs.Name: SizeIn
Inputs.Description: Task Image input size in bytes.

Inputs.Name: SizeOut
Inputs.Description: Task Image output size in bytes.

Inputs.Name: PointerName
Inputs.Description: Interface pointer name to bind on the instance.

Inputs.Name: EnsureDefaultTaskVariables
Inputs.Description: Whether to regenerate default Inputs/Outputs Vars groups.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm Task Image plus instance InterfacePointerValues/IoTaskImage were updated.

Steps.Summary: Creates or updates a root Mappings/MappingInfo entry.
Preconditions: Root Mappings will be created if missing.
Identifier and Id must be known from TwinCAT-generated or documented topology.

Inputs.Name: Attributes
Inputs.Description: Optional additional MappingInfo attributes; Identifier and Id are owned by dedicated fields.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify root Mappings contains the requested MappingInfo Identifier/Id pair exactly once.

Steps.Summary: Ensures a deterministic Mapping OwnerA/OwnerB/Link triple exists without replacing unrelated mappings.
Preconditions: Owner names and variable names must match TwinCAT path/value expectations.

Inputs.Name: OwnerAName
Inputs.Description: OwnerA Name attribute, for example TIXC^Demo^ObjA.

Inputs.Name: OwnerBName
Inputs.Description: OwnerB Name attribute, for example TIXC^Demo^ObjB.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm Mappings contains the requested OwnerA/OwnerB/Link entry.

Steps.Summary: Upserts a parameter default under TmcDesc/ParameterValues for an instance.
Preconditions: The target instance must already exist in the .tsproj.
VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the Value node now exists with the expected child elements.

Steps.Summary: Ensures Plc/Project/Instance nodes exist for a named PLC project and instance.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify Plc/Project(Name)/Instance(Name) exists for the requested names.

Steps.Summary: Ensures a named Vars group exists under a PLC Instance with deterministic Var definitions.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project and PLC Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

Inputs.Name: GroupName
Inputs.Description: Vars group Name value, for example PlcTask Outputs.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceExistingGroup
Inputs.Description: Whether to replace an existing Vars group with the same Name.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the PLC instance Vars group attributes and Var entries.

Steps.Summary: Ensures typed System/Settings values such as Cpu and IoIdleTask without replacing the whole Settings section.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
At least one typed setting must be provided.

Inputs.Name: InsertBeforeTasks
Inputs.Description: Whether to insert Settings before System/Tasks when Settings is created and Tasks exists.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify System/Settings contains the requested Cpu and IoIdleTask attributes while existing Tasks remain intact.

Steps.Summary: Creates or updates the declarative task node inside a .tsproj generated by XAE.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the Task attributes match the requested values.

Steps.Summary: Ensures a Task Image entry exists with deterministic Id, size, and addressing attributes.
Preconditions: The target Task node must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceExistingImage
Inputs.Description: Whether existing Task Image nodes should be replaced.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the target Task contains the expected Image attributes and Name value.

Steps.Summary: Ensures a PLC TaskPouOid entry exists with the requested priority and optional OTCID.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project and PLC Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm TaskPouOids/TaskPouOid has the requested Prio and OTCID.

Steps.Summary: Ensures a named Vars group exists under a Task with deterministic variable shape and addressing.
Preconditions: The target Task node must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: GroupName
Inputs.Description: Vars group Name element value, for example Inputs.

Inputs.Name: BaseVarName
Inputs.Description: Base text used for generated Var names.

Inputs.Name: TypeName
Inputs.Description: TwinCAT variable type, for example REAL.

Inputs.Name: Count
Inputs.Description: Number of Var nodes to generate.

Inputs.Name: BitStride
Inputs.Description: Bit offset stride between generated Vars.

Inputs.Name: ExternalAddressStride
Inputs.Description: ExternalAddress increment per generated Var after the first.

Inputs.Name: StartIndex
Inputs.Description: Starting suffix index for generated Var names.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceExistingGroup
Inputs.Description: Whether to replace existing Vars groups with the same Name.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the target Task has the expected Vars group name, variable count, and field values.

Steps.Summary: Merges a captured XML fragment into a named container, acting as the escape hatch only for remaining TwinCAT XML gaps without a dedicated primitive.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
Use a dedicated tsproj primitive instead when one exists for the same intent.
The fragment must come from a known-good .tsproj source, with documented target parent path, field meanings, source evidence, and test or real-machine evidence.
The target parent local name must be unambiguous in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: MatchElementName
Inputs.Description: Local element name used to find and replace an existing sibling.

Inputs.Name: MatchNameValue
Inputs.Description: Optional <Name> value used to find the existing sibling.

Inputs.Name: ReplaceExisting
Inputs.Description: Whether a matched sibling should be replaced.

Inputs.Name: TargetParentPath
Inputs.Description: Human-readable target parent path/layer in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: FieldMeaning
Inputs.Description: Short explanation of the fragment fields and why they are required.

Inputs.Name: VerificationEvidence
Inputs.Description: Test, XAE reopen/build/activation, or dated evidence proving this fragment is safe enough to use.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and compare the target container before/after the merge.
For engineering behavior, follow with XAE reopen/build/activation or a dated evidence note explaining why runtime proof is not available.

Steps.Summary: Refreshes existing C++ instance TmcDesc metadata from the project .tmc while preserving instance context and value sections.
Preconditions: The target C++ project instances must already exist in the .tsproj.
The project .tmc must contain the requested module class names.

Inputs.Name: CppProjectName
Inputs.Description: Owning C++ project name in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: ProjectTmcPath
Inputs.Description: Absolute path to the project .tmc file.

Inputs.Name: PreserveValueSections
Inputs.Description: Preserve existing ParameterValues, InterfacePointerValues, and DataPointerValues.

Inputs.Name: PreserveContextValues
Inputs.Description: Preserve existing TmcDesc Contexts and task ManualConfig.

Inputs.Name: ImportDataTypesFromTmc
Inputs.Description: Replace root .tsproj DataTypes from the project .tmc DataTypes.

Inputs.Name: FailIfMissingModule
Inputs.Description: Fail when an instance mapping references a missing TMC module.

Outputs.Name: refreshedCount
Outputs.Description: Number of instance TmcDesc sections refreshed.

Outputs.Name: errorsJson
Outputs.Description: Detailed mismatch list when refresh fails.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm instance DataAreas/Parameters/Pointers match the project .tmc while prior values and task bindings remain.

Steps.Summary: Replaces the root DataTypes section with a caller-provided fragment.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
The fragment must be a valid DataTypes element.

Inputs.Name: DataTypesXml
Inputs.Description: DataTypes XML fragment to write, rooted at <DataTypes>.

Inputs.Name: InsertBeforeProject
Inputs.Description: Whether to place DataTypes before the root Project element when present.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify DataTypes appears in the expected root position with requested content.

Steps.Summary: Replaces the root-level Mappings section with a caller-provided fragment.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
The fragment must be a valid Mappings element.

Inputs.Name: MappingsXml
Inputs.Description: Mappings XML fragment to write, rooted at <Mappings>.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and compare the Mappings section against the requested fragment.

Steps.Summary: Replaces the root Project/Io section with a caller-provided Io fragment.
Preconditions: A root-level Project node must exist in the .tsproj.
The fragment must be a valid Io element.

Inputs.Name: IoXml
Inputs.Description: Io XML fragment to write, rooted at <Io>.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the root Project contains the requested Io section.

Steps.Summary: Replaces System/Settings with a caller-provided Settings fragment while preserving other System children.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
The fragment must be a valid Settings element.

Inputs.Name: SettingsXml
Inputs.Description: Settings XML fragment to write, rooted at <Settings>.

Inputs.Name: InsertBeforeTasks
Inputs.Description: Whether to insert Settings before System/Tasks when Tasks exists.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify System/Settings content and insertion position.

Steps.Summary: Updates C++ Instance metadata attributes such as Disabled, KeepUnrestoredLinks, ClassFactoryId, or ObjectId without replacing TmcDesc.
Preconditions: The target C++ Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: Disabled
Inputs.Description: Optional Disabled attribute value. false removes the Disabled attribute.

Inputs.Name: ObjectId
Inputs.Description: Optional instance Id/ObjectId attribute value, for example #x01010010.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the C++ Instance metadata attributes were applied while TmcDesc remains intact.

Steps.Summary: Updates Plc Instance metadata attributes and optional CLSID/ClassFactory fields without replacing unrelated nodes.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project and PLC Instance must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify PLC instance attributes and CLSID metadata values.

Steps.Summary: Updates Plc/Project attribute-level properties such as project paths, reload behavior, AMS port, and archive settings.
Preconditions: The target PLC Project must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: PlcProjectName
Inputs.Description: PLC project node name under Plc.

Inputs.Name: AmsPort
Inputs.Description: Optional PLC project AmsPort attribute value.

Inputs.Name: FileArchiveSettings
Inputs.Description: Optional FileArchiveSettings attribute value, for example #x0002.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the PLC Project attribute values were applied as requested.

Steps.Summary: Sets Task Affinity and/or AdtTasks attributes for scheduler placement-sensitive workloads.
Preconditions: The target Task node must already exist in the .tsproj.

Inputs.Name: Affinity
Inputs.Description: Optional TwinCAT affinity mask or list, for example 1 or 0,2. Omit to update only AdtTasks.

Inputs.Name: EnableAdtTasks
Inputs.Description: Whether to force AdtTasks=true on the task.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and confirm the Task node includes the requested Affinity and/or AdtTasks attributes.

Steps.Summary: Upserts a generic XML element under a path-based .tsproj parent using a declared conflict policy.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
Prefer dedicated tsproj primitives when one exists for the same intent.

Inputs.Name: ParentPath
Inputs.Description: Path from root to the parent element, optionally matching <Name> values.

Inputs.Name: ElementName
Inputs.Description: Element local name to insert or update.

Inputs.Name: MatchNameValue
Inputs.Description: Optional <Name> child value used to match an existing element.

Inputs.Name: Attributes
Inputs.Description: Attributes to write on the target element.

Inputs.Name: ChildValues
Inputs.Description: Simple child element values to write under the target element.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and verify the resolved parent contains exactly the intended element shape.

Steps.Summary: Upserts a generic XML fragment under a path-based .tsproj parent using a declared conflict policy.
Preconditions: The target .tsproj must already exist on disk.
The fragment must already be shaped like Beckhoff expects.

Inputs.Name: ParentPath
Inputs.Description: Path from root to the parent element, optionally matching <Name> values.

Inputs.Name: FragmentXml
Inputs.Description: XML fragment to insert or update.

Inputs.Name: MatchElementName
Inputs.Description: Local element name used to match an existing sibling.

Inputs.Name: MatchNameValue
Inputs.Description: Optional <Name> value used to match an existing sibling.

VerificationNotes: Re-open the .tsproj and compare the resolved parent before/after the fragment upsert.

Steps.Summary: Reads a PLC or TcCOM symbol over ADS so the engineering pipeline can close the loop with a runtime assertion.
Preconditions: The target runtime must be reachable over ADS.
The symbol path and AMS endpoint must already be known.

Inputs.Name: SymbolPath
Inputs.Description: Fully qualified PLC or TcCOM symbol name.

Inputs.Name: AutoReconnect
Inputs.Description: Whether to force a TwinCAT reconnect attempt before reading.

VerificationNotes: Treat a successful read plus a domain assertion on the returned value as the runtime acceptance check.

Steps.Summary: Reads multiple PLC or TcCOM symbols over ADS and prints their values as a runtime validation checkpoint.
Preconditions: The target runtime must be reachable over ADS.
The symbol paths, data types, and AMS endpoint must already be known.

Inputs.Name: Symbols
Inputs.Description: Symbols to read; direct CLI accepts --symbols=MAIN.nSeed:UInt32;MAIN.bPipelineOk:Boolean or --json-file.

Inputs.Name: AutoReconnect
Inputs.Description: Whether to force a TwinCAT reconnect attempt before reading.

Inputs.Name: ContinueOnError
Inputs.Description: Whether the step should succeed when one or more symbols fail.

Outputs.Name: failedCount
Outputs.Description: Number of symbols that failed to read.

Outputs.Name: valuesText
Outputs.Description: Human-readable symbol=value list printed in the step result.

Outputs.Name: valuesJson
Outputs.Description: Per-symbol ADS read results including data types and errors.

VerificationNotes: Use after activation and ADS port scan; treat the printed values plus expected domain assertions as runtime proof.

Steps.Summary: Scans ADS target ports and reports whether the runtime endpoint is reachable before symbol-level ADS reads are attempted.
Preconditions: TwinCAT runtime or router must be installed on the target machine.
Use this before symbol reads when activation or ADS routing is uncertain.

Inputs.Name: NetId
Inputs.Description: AMS NetId, or local for the local router.

Outputs.Name: openPortCount
Outputs.Description: Number of ports with readable ADS state.

Outputs.Name: failedPortCount
Outputs.Description: Number of ports that failed to connect or read state.

Outputs.Name: portsJson
Outputs.Description: Per-port ADS state or error message.

VerificationNotes: If 100 or 10000 are not reachable, treat later PLC symbol read failures as runtime/router/permission failures rather than symbol-path failures.
